k6 tests use port 8883 for localhost (dockerHosts). Since the Helm chart is for k8s deployment and uses NodePort 30080, I think it's better to keep the Helm chart consistent and update the k6 config to use 8080 instead.

NB: does the serviceCountName have anything to do with the init containers logic. I dont think so. Maybe move that property down to a more relevant area if you can. Or at some comment about it. The hazelcast setting seem very much out of place in this whole file...

The serviceAccountName applies to the entire pod (both init and main containers) s the convention is to position it at the pod spec level.

Yeah this one is on me. I just used some defaut times. I would suggest going back to the docker values for now at least unless we observe issues with them.
Also I would suggest looking at the right sizing to see if we can get solid numbers for cpu and memory for cps 
https://eteamspace.ericsson.net/display/RERIC/Right+Sizing

This comes from the _helpers.tpl. In this instance fullname represents the Release name + chart name.
These helpers ensure consistent naming across all Kubernetes resources and allow multiple chart installations in the same namespace without name conflicts.
No one else should be using our charts. We only have them for our own test purposes but if they are published I suppose people could

Like we have in docker:
    extends:
      service: cps-and-ncmp-template
The k8s equivalent of this is install instances in different releases.

There might be something cleverer we can do but it will need some investigtion.
The standard convention is to use postgresql
